mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS changes or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of automobiles and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above the 100 index baseline confirms capacity utilization expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable inflation expectations.
failure_condition: INDPRO above 100 yields negative or flat 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%, breaking the supply-demand equilibrium assumption.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity, which consistently translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days via improved corporate revenue forecasts.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward returns across all tested macro regimes, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings exceeding a 0.3 percent threshold trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.3% MoM are followed by positive 60-day returns in > 70% of cases, indicating markets price in inflation as benign or growth-driven.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly auto sales reveals underlying demand resilience; when RSAFS outpaces GDPC1 momentum, it signals consumer-led expansion driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The RSAFS-GDPC1 divergence metric shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.02 and direction accuracy at 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production contraction exceeding 1.0 percent indicates severe supply-side shock, prompting immediate market repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO drops > 1.0% MoM are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances, suggesting supply shocks are offset by monetary accommodation or inventory drawdowns.
